title: AI Crawler Ecosystem 2026: Who Scrapes the Web and Who Pays
description: Machines now generate 57.5% of web HTML traffic. Who the AI crawlers are, what they take per visitor sent, and how pay-per-crawl rewrites the web's deal.

key_facts.fact: Automated requests reached 57.5% of HTML web traffic versus 42.5% human — the first machine majority in web history
AI crawlers accounted for 20.3% of verified bot traffic in May 2026, with AI-search bots adding 6.5%
52.3% of AI crawler requests were for model training and only 2.6% were real-time user-initiated fetches in the 28 days to June 22, 2026
Anthropic's crawl-to-refer ratio fell from 73,000:1 (2025) to 11,122:1 (May 25–Jun 1, 2026) as Claude added search and citation features, but remains over 2,000x Google's 4.9:1
key_facts.source: Cloudflare via TechCrunch 2026-07-01; SEOmator GEO Data Report 2026-03-17 reading Cloudflare Radar

key_facts.fact: Cloudflare pay-per-crawl (launched 2025-07-01) answers crawlers with HTTP 402 and a crawler-price header; publishers choose allow, charge, or block per crawler
From September 15, 2026, Cloudflare default-blocks training and agent bots on ad-monetized pages of new domains; mixed-use crawlers that refuse to separate search from training face blocking
Cloudflare blocked 416 billion AI bot requests in the five months after July 2025, and over 2.5 million sites disallowed AI training
key_facts.source: Cloudflare / Matthew Prince via Search Engine Land, August 2025

key_facts.fact: Cloudflare documented Perplexity using undeclared stealth crawlers impersonating Chrome and rotating IPs/ASNs at 3–6 million requests per day, and delisted it as a verified bot

RSL (Really Simple Licensing), launched 2025-09-10 with a 1.0 spec in December 2025, adds machine-readable licensing terms (attribution, pay-per-crawl, pay-per-inference) on top of robots.txt but has no native enforcement
ClaudeBot gained 8 percentage points in one month to become the #2 AI-adjacent crawler at 20.0% share in June 2026, while Googlebot fell below 25% for the first time
key_facts.source: websearchapi.ai Monthly AI Crawler Report, June 8 – July 6, 2026 (third-party network measurement)

faq_summary.q: Should I block AI crawlers on my website?
faq_summary.a: Block by purpose, not by company: training bots return near-zero traffic, while search and user-action bots are how AI platforms cite and visit you, and AI referrals convert at a premium.

faq_summary.q: Does blocking GPTBot remove my site from ChatGPT search?
faq_summary.a: No. GPTBot gathers training data only; ChatGPT search visibility is governed by OAI-SearchBot, and ChatGPT-User fetches are not governed by robots.txt at all.
Pages crawled per human visitor referred back. Google search ran ~5–14:1 in 2025–2026, while AI companies ran from ~111:1 (Perplexity) to 11,122–73,000:1 (Anthropic).
Cloudflare's per-request payment system, live since July 1, 2025: sites answer crawlers with HTTP 402 plus a price header, and publishers choose allow, charge, or block per bot.

faq_summary.q: Do AI crawlers actually respect robots.txt?
faq_summary.a: Declared bots from major labs document compliance, but robots.txt is voluntary — Cloudflare caught Perplexity using stealth crawlers in August 2025, and user-action fetchers bypass robots.txt by design.

entries.respects_robots_txt: declared yes; stealth-crawling dispute documented by Cloudflare 2025-08

entries.declared_purpose: validates pages submitted as ChatGPT ads
